sidebar.noCollectionsHint: Click "New" above to start building your API workspace
sidebar.noHistoryHint: Sent requests will leave traces here
sidebar.envVariablesHint: Click "Add" to create the first environment

tcpudp.title: TCP / UDP Debug Workbench
tcpudp.description: Supports TCP Client/Server and UDP Client/Server modes for real-time packet transmission with HEX/ASCII data visualization, ideal for IoT and network protocol debugging.

capture.title: Traffic Capture Proxy
capture.description: Local HTTP proxy-based traffic capture tool for real-time interception and analysis of network requests, with HTTPS decryption, filtering, and detail inspection for API debugging and security auditing.

loadtest.title: HTTP Load Test Engine
loadtest.description: High-concurrency HTTP load testing engine with configurable concurrency, duration, and RPS limiting. Real-time visualization of throughput, latency distribution, and error rates for API performance evaluation.

toolWorkbench.dragToDetachSession: Drag this tab to detach as a separate window
toolWorkbench.allSessionsDetached: All instances are detached into separate windows

welcome.title: Pick a feature to get started
welcome.subtitle: ProtoForge covers HTTP, WebSocket, GraphQL and more, plus traffic capture, load testing, and low-level networking tools.
welcome.requestFeaturesDesc: REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, SSE, and MQTT — all in one place
welcome.toolFeaturesDesc: Low-level networking, traffic analysis, and performance testing
welcome.newRequestDesc: Create an HTTP request and debug API endpoints
welcome.commandPaletteDesc: Search and quickly execute any action
welcome.preferencesDesc: Customize theme, proxy, and data policies
welcome.httpRequestDesc: Full REST method support with params, headers, body, auth, and scripts
welcome.graphqlDesc: Dedicated Query / Mutation / Variables editor with schema-driven debugging
welcome.websocketDesc: Bidirectional real-time debugging with auto-reconnect, heartbeat, and custom headers
welcome.sseDesc: Real-time monitoring and analysis of server-sent event streams
welcome.tcpudpDesc: Client and server modes with HEX / ASCII / Base64 encoding
welcome.captureDesc: Local HTTP proxy capture with HTTPS decryption and request detail analysis
welcome.loadtestDesc: High-concurrency load testing with real-time RPS, latency, and error rate visualization
welcome.pluginSystemDesc: Extend capabilities with protocol parsing, data generation, and custom rendering plugins

http.noAuth: This request does not carry any authentication.
http.requestFailedDesc: The target service could not be reached. Check whether the service is running, the host/port is correct, and the current proxy or network settings are valid.
http.variableNoCollection: Save the request into a collection before editing collection variables
http.variableDynamicReadonly: Dynamic variables are computed at send time and cannot be edited directly.
http.readyDesc: Enter a URL and click Send to debug your API
http.fileBinaryDesc: File will be sent as binary

graphql.modeDesc: Reuse the same HTTP URL, headers and auth settings while focusing the editor on Query and Variables
graphql.title: GraphQL Request Body
graphql.desc: Query is sent as the body; Variables are automatically combined with Query into a standard GraphQL JSON payload
graphql.queryDesc: Write your query or mutation body
graphql.variablesOptionalDetail: Query will be sent without variables if left empty
graphql.variablesValidDetail: These fields will be combined with the query into a GraphQL payload
graphql.variablesInvalidDetail: Variables must be a valid JSON object

oauth2.authorizingHint: A browser window has been opened. Please complete the login and authorization. The window will close automatically.
oauth2.accessTokenPlaceholder: Click "Get Token" to auto-fill, or paste manually

script.helperText: Tab indent supported · Use Snippets to quickly insert templates
script.preScriptHelper: Executes before the request is sent
script.postScriptHelper: Executes after the response is received

http.binaryDesc: File will be sent as binary

saveDialog.title: Save Request
saveDialog.noCollections: No collections yet, create one first

ws.reconnectResetDesc: Reset reconnect counter on successful connection
ws.emptyDesc: Connect to a server to start sending and receiving messages
ws.detailEmptyTitle: Select a message to view details
ws.detailEmptyDesc: Click an event above to inspect it as JSON, text, or hex.

sse.urlPlaceholder: Enter SSE endpoint URL, e.g. https://api.example.com/events
sse.emptyDesc: Events will appear here after connecting to an SSE endpoint

mqtt.subscriptionHint: Subscribe to multiple topics after connecting. Supports `+` and `#` wildcards.
mqtt.publishDesc: Publish a message to the target topic
mqtt.emptyDesc: Messages will appear here after connecting to an MQTT broker and subscribing to topics

modes.tcpClientHint: Connection-oriented reliable data transfer for scenarios requiring data integrity
modes.tcpServerHint: Listen on a specified port to accept client connections and handle data exchange
modes.udpClientHint: Connectionless fast packet transfer for latency-sensitive scenarios
modes.udpServerHint: Listen on a UDP port to receive and respond to datagrams

tcp.replyAddrPlaceholder: Auto-filled after receiving data, or enter IP:Port manually

sendPanel.title: Send Panel
sendPanel.textPlaceholder: Enter data, Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline
sendPanel.description: Send data in ASCII / HEX / Base64 encoding formats
sendPanel.newCommandHint: Save common payloads as quick commands and load them in one click later.
sendPanel.quickCommandEmpty: No quick commands yet — click to add a common payload
sendPanel.broadcastHint: When no client is selected, the server broadcasts the payload to all online connections.
sendPanel.unicastHint: The payload will be sent only to the selected client.
sendPanel.directTargetHint: The payload will be sent to the current connection or target address.
sendPanel.replyHint: The payload will be sent back to the current source address.

messageLog.title: Message Log
messageLog.emptyListening: Server started, waiting for client connections
messageLog.emptyNotListening: Configure port and click "Listen" to start the server
messageLog.noMatchHint: Try shorter keywords or switch display format before filtering.
messageLog.emptyConnectedDesc: Connection is ready. Sent and received messages will appear here in chronological order with byte statistics and source info.
messageLog.emptyDisconnectedDesc: Verify address and port, then click connect. Once connected, this becomes the real-time message log for the session.
messageLog.step1ConnectedDesc: The link is ready. You can start testing with ASCII, HEX, or Base64 data.
messageLog.step1DisconnectedDesc: Check host, port, and mode to avoid connecting to the wrong target.
messageLog.step2ConnectedDesc: The send panel supports timed sends, history, and quick commands — try a handshake or heartbeat first.
messageLog.step2DisconnectedDesc: After connecting, the client initiates a session; server/UDP modes start listening for incoming data.
messageLog.step3ConnectedDesc: Received data is listed chronologically. Use the toolbar to search, switch format, or clear the log.
messageLog.step3DisconnectedDesc: Once connected, sent/received data, size statistics, and source addresses appear here in real-time.

capture.emptyDesc: Proxy traffic will appear here in chronological order
capture.emptyStep1Desc: Confirm the listening port, then click Start Capture
capture.emptyStep2Desc: Point your browser or system proxy to the local port
capture.emptyStep3Desc: Filter, inspect headers, request body and response metadata
capture.noRequestSelected: Select a request from the left to view details
capture.noPluginsHint: Install protocol parser plugins to enhance analysis
capture.proxyHint: Point your browser or system HTTP proxy to this address, and requests will appear in the list above in real time
capture.httpsHint: To capture HTTPS, export and trust the CA certificate first, then refresh your browser or target application
capture.emptyState: Click "Start Capture" to launch the local HTTP proxy, then configure your browser proxy to capture traffic
capture.bodyNotSupported: Response body capture is not supported (only Headers and metadata are recorded)
capture.openBrowserHint: Set system proxy and open in default browser, auto-restore when capture stops
capture.browserProxyNotRunning: Please start the capture proxy first
capture.caCertTitle: Trust CA Certificate for HTTPS Capture
capture.caCertDesc: The capture proxy uses a self-signed CA certificate to decrypt HTTPS traffic. You need to install this certificate into your system trust store, otherwise browsers and apps will reject connections.
capture.caNotTrustedTitle: ⚠️ Install CA Certificate for HTTPS Capture
capture.caNotTrustedDesc: ProtoForge CA certificate is not installed in your system. HTTPS sites cannot be captured without it (browsers will reject the proxy certificate). Click to install automatically.
capture.caTrustedDesc: ProtoForge CA certificate is installed and trusted. HTTPS traffic can be captured normally.

loadtest.title: HTTP Load Test
loadtest.configDesc: Configure method, concurrency, end condition, timeout and rate limiting
loadtest.noRateLimitDesc: No rate limit enabled, requests will be sent at maximum throughput
loadtest.bodyEmptyDesc: Leave empty when no body is needed
loadtest.emptyDesc: Configure target URL, concurrency and duration, then click Start Test
loadtest.emptyTargetDesc: Enter URL and select the HTTP method to test
loadtest.emptyConcurrencyDesc: Set concurrency, duration or request count
loadtest.emptyResultDesc: Shows RPS, latency distribution, error rate and status codes
loadtest.bodyEmptyHint: Leave empty when no body is needed; switch to JSON or RAW to add request payload
loadtest.authEmptyHint: No authentication for this request; switch to Bearer or Basic for authenticated endpoints

settings.title: Preferences
settings.subtitle: Manage theme, request behavior, proxy connections, and local data policies.
settings.categoryNavDesc: Select the workbench area to adjust. Settings will appear on the right.
settings.resetConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset all settings to their default values?

sections.dataDesc: History, saving, and local data policies

general.themeDesc: Control the color scheme and light/dark mode behavior.
general.fontSizeDesc: Adjust the default font size for the editor and interface.
general.fontFamilyDesc: Default font family for code areas.
general.checkUpdateDesc: Current version v{{version}}. Checks GitHub Release for newer versions.

request.timeoutDesc: Default timeout for HTTP requests. Can be overridden per request.
request.followRedirectsDesc: Automatically follow HTTP 3xx redirects when sending requests.
request.maxRedirectsDesc: Prevent infinite redirects. An error will occur if exceeded.
request.sslVerifyDesc: Disable to access self-signed HTTPS endpoints. Not recommended for production.
request.autoSaveCookiesDesc: Automatically save response cookies for subsequent requests.

proxy.enableDesc: Send HTTP requests through a proxy server.
proxy.typeDesc: Select the proxy protocol for the current request pipeline.
proxy.portDesc: Port number used by the proxy service.
proxy.authDesc: Enable if the proxy requires username and password.

data.maxHistoryDesc: Oldest history entries will be automatically cleaned when the limit is exceeded.
data.autoSaveIntervalDesc: Periodically save current request content. 0 disables auto-save.

plugin.centerDesc: Browse, install and manage extension plugins
plugin.protocolParserDesc: Parse raw packets into structured data
plugin.requestHookDesc: Sign, encrypt, and inject before/after requests
plugin.responseRendererDesc: Custom rendering for charts, HEX, tree views
plugin.dataGeneratorDesc: Mock data, random values, template filling
plugin.exportFormatDesc: Export to cURL, HTTPie, code snippets
plugin.installFromStore: Install plugins from the store to extend functionality
plugin.trySwitchCategory: Try selecting another category or clearing search
plugin.aboutTip1: Plugins are downloaded from remote registry with hot-reload
plugin.aboutTip2: All plugins run in a sandbox without affecting core functionality
plugin.aboutTip3: Uninstalling a plugin also removes its local data

import.title: Import Collection
import.subtitle: Quickly generate request workspace from local collection files or OpenAPI documents
import.openApiImportDesc: Swagger / OpenAPI URL discovery & interface preview
import.sourceDesc: Select import method; the right panel will show the corresponding configuration and preview flow
import.selectFile: Select Postman or ProtoForge Collection File
import.fromFileDesc: Select a local JSON file; the system will auto-detect Postman Collection or ProtoForge format
import.importingDesc: Parsing and writing collection data, please wait
import.importDoneDesc: Collection imported successfully, dialog will close shortly
import.supportedFormats: Supports Postman v2.0/v2.1 and ProtoForge JSON; imported data auto-syncs to sidebar
import.modeCreateDesc: Create a new collection and import all content
import.modeMergeDesc: Compare with an existing collection and only add new or changed endpoints
import.noApiFound: No API groups or documents found
import.fromOpenApiDesc: Enter Swagger / OpenAPI URL to auto-detect groups and preview before importing
import.urlInputDesc: Enter document URL; supports doc.html, swagger-ui pages or raw spec URLs
import.groupSelectDesc: Select groups to import; uncached groups will be fetched automatically
import.waitDetectDesc: After entering URL, the system will auto-detect groups, parse endpoints and generate importable preview
import.endpointPreviewDesc: Browse endpoints by tag and control the final import items
import.noMatchEndpointsDesc: Try adjusting search keywords or reselecting API groups to import
import.importAutoCreate: A collection will be auto-created with the selected endpoints on import
import.fetchingGroupsDesc: Fetching endpoint details for selected groups, please wait
import.waitPreviewDesc: After document detection, grouped endpoint preview and import selection will appear here

collectionSettings.title: Collection Settings
collectionSettings.notFound: Collection does not exist or has been deleted
collectionSettings.name: Collection Name
collectionSettings.descPlaceholder: Describe the purpose of this collection...
collectionSettings.varsDesc: Collection-level variables can be referenced in all requests using {{key}}

env.title: Environment Management
env.selectEnvHint: Or create a new one from the left panel
env.globalVarsDesc: Global variables are available in all environments with lower priority than environment variables

runner.emptyDesc: Select requests to run, set iterations and delay

response.invalidJsonPrettyFallback: The response is marked as JSON, but it could not be parsed. Showing the raw content below.

update.checkingDesc: Connecting to GitHub Release for the latest version…
update.upToDateDesc: You are on the latest version. No update needed.
update.clickToUpdate: New version v{{version}} available, click to update
